title: RUSTY Review Round 2: full project
notes:
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — The code is correct, well-structured, and has good test coverage. The core architecture (three-layer lexer/form/evaluator) is sound and idiomatic. Main weaknesses are: the duplicated clause-scan state machine in lib.rs, the repeated Vec<&Form> chaining pattern in the test evaluators, and a few readability gaps around invariants. No unsafe code, no logic errors found.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: the rposition semantics in validate_regex_in_clause (could be a deeper correctness issue, not just readability), and the str_contains Octet early-return ordering (correct but slightly surprising). Uncertainty driven by: not running the test suite myself to confirm all edge cases pass, and not having RFC 5228 text open to verify every semantic claim in comments. Would update assessment if: the test suite fails on any of the flagged areas, or if a RFC 5228 expert identifies the rposition usage as incorrect for multi-argument test forms with more than two string-list args.
